sections.section_title: About the PCI Security Standards Council

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for assessing and validating compliance with the PCI DSS. It is essential for ensuring that all entities involved in payment card processing adhere to the established security standards. The policy provides a framework for utilizing Qualified Security Assessors (QSAs) and Approved Scanning Vendors (ASVs) to facilitate compliance assessments.
policies.policy_text: All organizations that accept, process, store, or transmit credit card information must undergo regular compliance assessments with PCI DSS. Organizations must engage QSAs for comprehensive assessments and ASVs for vulnerability scans of their Internet-facing environments. The Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) must be completed by eligible organizations that are not required to submit a Report on Compliance (ROC).
policies.scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including merchants, service providers, and third-party vendors. It covers all systems and applications that handle credit card information, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all entities comply with PCI DSS requirements through regular assessments and validations. This includes achieving measurable compliance outcomes, reducing risks associated with payment card data, and ensuring that all personnel involved in payment processing are aware of compliance requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: This subpolicy details the requirements for engaging QSAs to assess compliance with PCI DSS. It outlines the process for selecting and working with QSAs to ensure thorough evaluations of compliance status.
subpolicies.control: Organizations must engage a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) to conduct a comprehensive assessment of PCI DSS compliance. The engagement should occur annually or whenever significant changes to the payment environment occur. The QSA must be selected based on their qualifications and experience in PCI DSS assessments. The assessment results must be documented and reviewed by senior management to ensure compliance status is understood and addressed.

compliances.Description: Organizations must engage a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) to conduct a comprehensive assessment of PCI DSS compliance. The engagement should occur annually or whenever significant changes to the payment environment occur.
